amendments.normalized_body: The `debug_assert!` in `unique_terminal_replay_match` (fs.rs:2824) only fires in debug builds. In release builds, calling `(Some(run_id), None)` silently bypasses the started_at guard on runless terminal candidates, allowing backfill onto the wrong legacy row. Replace `debug_assert!` with a runtime check that returns `None` (or panics) to enforce the contract in all build modes.

The plan_hash auto-population from snapshot in `record_task_run_start` (fs.rs:3017-3019) runs unconditionally for new entries, but the comment block at fs.rs:2988-2992 explains that snapshot-based backfill is intentionally NOT applied to existing rows because persist_plan() can advance the snapshot hash after the original attempt started. However, the same race applies to new entries: if a plan is persisted between the bead being dispatched and `record_task_run_start` being called, the new entry gets stamped with the newer plan hash even though the bead was dispatched under the older plan. Consider documenting this known limitation in the code comment, or having the caller always supply plan_hash explicitly when it knows which plan version dispatched the bead.

src/adapters/fs.rs:2976-2991 and src/adapters/fs.rs:3297-3319 still leave `plan_hash` unset on existing lineage rows when callers omit it. New rows use `snapshot_plan_hash()`, but replaying or finalizing a preexisting row only copies an explicit caller-supplied `plan_hash` and then explicitly skips snapshot backfill. A legacy or partial row created with `plan_hash=None` therefore stays unlabeled forever even when the milestone snapshot already has the canonical hash, so issue 3 is still unimplemented for repair/replay paths. Please add a safe backfill path for existing rows as well, or persist enough provenance to distinguish plan evolution before deciding to skip it.

src/adapters/fs.rs:3059-3098 calls `unique_terminal_replay_match()` with every row for the same bead/project, and src/adapters/fs.rs:2859-2862 only accepts a candidate if every other row is a legacy start pair for that candidate. That makes named terminal replays fail as soon as the same bead/project has any other completed attempt: a runless terminal row for attempt A cannot be repaired/backfilled after later attempt B exists, even when `started_at` uniquely identifies attempt A. Filter the terminal fallback by `started_at` (or otherwise exclude unrelated attempts) before invoking the uniqueness check.

src/adapters/fs.rs:3059-3098 calls `unique_terminal_replay_match()` with every row for the same bead/project, and src/adapters/fs.rs:2859-2862 only accepts a candidate if every other row is a legacy start pair for that candidate. payload.summary: Final review accepted 4 amendment(s); restart required.
